multi_model_confirmed.title: HALTED/SKIPPED Semantics Contract Broken
multi_model_confirmed.description: The HALTED vs SKIPPED status contract is inconsistently defined across TOPOLOGY, bridge-commons, and individual bridge files. bridge-commons defines HALTED as 'wait; do not continue', but deep-council silently auto-SKIPs HALTED bridges in non-interactive mode. The conversion policy exists only in deep-council prose and is absent from bridge-commons, TOPOLOGY, and council-report-schema.json.
multi_model_confirmed.evidence: bridge-commons HALTED vs SKIPPED table; TOPOLOGY lines 25-26; bridge-codex Step 2; bridge-opencode Step 2; deep-council Step 5 non-interactive default; council-report-schema.json (no auto_skipped_halted_bridges field)
multi_model_confirmed.remediation: Add HALTED→SKIPPED conversion policy to bridge-commons. Add auto_skipped_halted_bridges as a required field in council-report-schema.json and Step 7 template. Align TOPOLOGY.

multi_model_confirmed.title: Stateless CLI Context Overflow
multi_model_confirmed.description: CLI bridges (Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, OpenCode CLI) must embed all previous-round findings in Round N prompts. No size limit, summarization strategy, or file-based injection path exists. Silent truncation is indistinguishable from valid partial responses.
multi_model_confirmed.evidence: bridge-commons Context Passing Between Rounds (stateless paths); bridge-gemini Execution; bridge-codex Step 3B; bridge-opencode Step 3B
multi_model_confirmed.remediation: Add maximum context size constant to bridge-commons. Require stateless bridges to summarize prior-round findings above threshold before embedding.

multi_model_confirmed.title: Bridge Input Alias Fragmentation
multi_model_confirmed.description: session_id/scope are canonical in bridge-commons but review_id/review_scope aliases persist in bridge templates without a centralized deprecation policy. The aliasing relationship is documented in deep-council Step 4 comment, but no deprecation timeline or removal path exists.
multi_model_confirmed.evidence: deep-council Step 4 Bridge Input comment; bridge-commons Input Schema; bridge-codex SKILL.md templates
multi_model_confirmed.remediation: Define deprecation timeline for review_id/review_scope aliases in bridge-commons.

multi_model_confirmed.title: TTL Logic (self-correcting by design)
multi_model_confirmed.description: TTL is checked at discovery phase only and uses a stored timestamp. DA challenge confirmed the mechanism is functional: (current_time - updated) > ttl_hours is a real-time comparison. Committed past timestamps self-expire on next run.
multi_model_confirmed.remediation: Add clarifying comment to .bridge-settings.json schema docs about TTL behavior.

single_source_findings.title: Preflight Threshold Contradiction
single_source_findings.description: preflight/SKILL.md says 'two or more signals unresolved' triggers preflight. All calling skills say 'one or more signals unresolved'. Directly contradictory threshold definitions in co-authoritative documents.

single_source_findings.title: Missing 'audit' task_type in bridge-commons
single_source_findings.description: bridge-commons task_type enum excludes 'audit'. Audit-intent tasks default to 'review' framing with review verdict thresholds, miscalibrating compliance audit results.

single_source_findings.title: Gemini Bridge Has No Auth Check
single_source_findings.description: bridge-gemini has no auth or quota probe beyond which gemini, while bridge-codex and bridge-opencode both perform explicit auth checks. A broken gemini installation produces SKIPPED at execution rather than surfacing the gap at availability check.

single_source_findings.title: Duplicate Step 2 in bridge-opencode
single_source_findings.description: bridge-opencode/SKILL.md has two sections labeled Step 2. Structural documentation defect.

single_source_findings.title: Model Identifiers in .bridge-settings.json May Be Invalid
single_source_findings.description: Committed model identifiers (gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gpt-5.3-codex) are not confirmed valid. Per-model failures degrade coverage silently.

integration_findings.title: auto_skipped_halted_bridges Missing from Output Schema
integration_findings.description: deep-council Step 5 introduces auto_skipped_halted_bridges in prose but the field is absent from council-report-schema.json and the Step 7 JSON template. Council verdicts can silently reflect fewer bridges than the user expects with no machine-readable partial-coverage flag.
integration_findings.evidence: deep-council Step 5 non-interactive default; council-report-schema.json required fields; Step 7 JSON template
integration_findings.remediation: Add auto_skipped_halted_bridges and partial_coverage fields to council-report-schema.json and Step 7 template.

integration_findings.title: Context Truncation Inflates Multi-Model Confirmation Confidence
integration_findings.description: Stateless CLI context overflow (MMC002) and Stage A 70% overlap threshold interact: two bridges that both truncate to the same recent context window can produce false multi-model agreement. Stage A treats truncation-survivor agreement as genuine independent convergence.
integration_findings.evidence: bridge-commons Context Passing Between Rounds; deep-council Stage A deduplication threshold; bridge output schema (no truncation_detected field)
integration_findings.remediation: Add shared_cli_path flag to Stage A. Require bridges to report approximate prompt size per round.

integration_findings.title: audit task_type Gap Miscalibrates Verdict Thresholds
integration_findings.description: SS004 and bridge-commons Verdict Logic combine: audit-intent runs dispatched as task_type review use review verdict thresholds calibrated for findings, not compliance requirements. A MEDIUM compliance gap may warrant FAIL under audit framing but produces CONCERNS under review framing.
integration_findings.evidence: bridge-commons task_type enum; bridge-commons Verdict Logic; deep-council Step 1 task_type default
integration_findings.remediation: Add 'audit' task_type to bridge-commons with compliance-calibrated verdict logic.

integration_findings.title: Preflight Threshold Mismatch Propagates to All Bridge Task Prompts
integration_findings.description: When deep-council triggers preflight under the 'one or more' threshold but preflight's spec would self-terminate at 'two or more', preflight runs in an under-determined state and may return assumption-filled scope. This underdetermined working_scope flows into bridge_input for all four bridge Task prompts simultaneously.
integration_findings.evidence: preflight/SKILL.md When to Invoke; deep-council Step 0; bridge_input construction Step 4
integration_findings.remediation: Align thresholds. Add scope_clarification.confidence validation in deep-council Step 0 post-preflight.

positions.position: MUST obligations in bridge-commons have no enforcement mechanism; weaker forms (schema validation, field presence checks) are implementable

positions.da_challenge: LLM compliance verification is a fundamental epistemic ceiling, not a fixable design gap; adding runtime checks would require a meta-agent subject to the same uncertainty

positions.position: Collapsing action-oriented instructions and background context into the same value weakens framing fidelity

positions.da_challenge: Redundancy is not destructive; agents receive the value in two labelled prompt slots (TASK: and CONTEXT:)

withdrawn_findings.title: No Session-Level Idempotency
withdrawn_findings.reason: Timestamp-scoped filenames prevent file collision by design; premise was factually incorrect

withdrawn_findings.title: Debate Coordinator SPOF
withdrawn_findings.reason: Every Task agent spawn carries equivalent SPOF risk; no architectural distinction

withdrawn_findings.title: Single Agent Performing Both DA and IC Roles
withdrawn_findings.reason: All bridge SKILL.md files explicitly define separate DA and IC agents; finding had no textual support

withdrawn_findings.title: Bridge Control-Plane Cascade (standalone)
withdrawn_findings.reason: Subsumed into INT001 which provides a more precise formulation with specific schema evidence

synthesis_notes: All four bridges returned CONCERNS independently. No CRITICAL findings. Architecture is coherent; the two-layer debate design, bridge-commons contract, and domain-registry routing are sound. The HALTED/SKIPPED semantic inconsistency (MMC001, 4/4 bridges) is the primary structural gap. Context overflow risk (MMC002 + INT002) affects the reliability of multi-model confirmation itself. Three findings were withdrawn by DA challenge, demonstrating the suite's own debate protocol functioning correctly in this audit.
